questions.prompt_html:
Solve the problem.

The unit vectors u and v are combined to produce two new vectors  and  Show that a and b are orthogonal. Assume
questions.prompt_text: Solve the problem. The unit vectors u and v are combined to produce two new vectors a = u + v and b = u - v. Show that a and b are orthogonal. Assume u ≠ v.
questions.solution_html:
u = ux i + uyj and v = vx i + vyj , so
a = u + v = (ux + vx) i + (uy + vy)j and b = u - v = (ux - vx) i + (uy - vy)j

Take the dot product a ∙ b:
a ∙ b = (u + v) ∙ (u - v) = (ux + vx)(ux - vx) + (uy + vy)(uy - vy)
 =  -  +  -   = ( + ) - (  + )
 =  -   = 1 - 1 = 0
Since the dot product of the two non-zero vectors is zero they are orthogonal.
